query: The user asks for a draft standup update. Which remembered preference should shape the answer?

context.title: Synthetic Memory: Communication Preference
context.text: User preference: Standup updates should be concise, teammate-like, and focused on what changed, what is blocked, and what is next. Avoid report-like phrasing and long background sections.

context.title: Synthetic Memory: Deployment Note
context.text: Project note: A deploy failed last week because the staging environment used an old API token. The token was rotated and the runbook was updated.

expected_answer: Use the concise, teammate-like standup preference: say what changed, what is blocked, and what is next, without long background or report-like prose.

query: A teammate asks what data source the local benchmark should use before any GPU spend. Which memory item answers that?

context.title: Synthetic Memory: Local-First Benchmark Rule
context.text: Workspace rule: Before spending on remote GPUs, the benchmark harness must run end-to-end against a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint, such as Ollama or llama.cpp. Remote runs are only for validated GPU measurements after the local path works.

context.title: Synthetic Memory: Plot Rule
context.text: Workspace rule: Plots must be generated by scripts from JSONL traces. Hand-made charts are not acceptable for benchmark claims.

expected_answer: Use the local-first rule: run the harness end-to-end against a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint such as Ollama or llama.cpp before spending on remote GPU runs.

query: Two memories conflict about how to answer architecture questions. Which one should win for a current implementation review?

context.title: Synthetic Memory: Old Architecture Style
context.text: Older preference: Architecture answers can start with a broad conceptual map before discussing code details.

context.title: Synthetic Memory: Current Source-Grounded Style
context.text: Current preference: For repo work, inspect the existing implementation first and ground recommendations in concrete files, tests, and observed behavior before abstract design advice.

expected_answer: The current source-grounded preference should win: inspect the implementation and anchor recommendations in concrete files, tests, and observed behavior before offering abstract architecture advice.
